transformations.input: Aluminosilicate powders (kaolinite/feldspar from mineral sands) + alkaline activators (NaOH + sodium silicate from seawater and quartz)
transformations.process: Geopolymerisation (mix, cast or 3D print, ambient/steam cure), optional basalt/bio-fibre reinforcement
outputs: Geopolymer concrete for foundations, walls, marine reef modules and wave-energy structures
byproducts: Minimal; far lower process CO₂ than Portland cement
transformations.byproductFate: Its activator demand is met by the sodium-silicate leachate from zircon processing and the caustic from chlor-alkali, so upstream 'wastes' become the binder chemistry; calcium-carbonate additive versions double as marine-reef substrate.
transformations.precedent: Fly-ash/metakaolin geopolymer concrete is an established low-carbon cement
transformations.caveat: Metakaolin (calcined kaolinite) is the reactive precursor; raw feldspar is comparatively unreactive, so 'feldspar' as a geopolymer feed is optimistic without calcination/activation.

transformations.process: Pyrolysis ~500°C in the absence of oxygen
byproducts: None treated as waste: all three fractions are used
transformations.byproductFate: This is the central nothing-wasted hub. Biochar becomes: the carbon reductant for silicon carbothermic reduction, high-purity graphite for Li-ion anodes, carbon black pigment, activated-carbon filtration media, carbon-fibre precursor, and soil amendment/carbon sequestration. Syngas is burned for heat and power. Bio-oil is refined to fuels and chemical precursors.
transformations.precedent: Biomass pyrolysis / biochar is an established process
transformations.caveat: The individual downstream uses vary in maturity (soil amendment and syngas power are routine; biochar-to-battery-graphite, biochar-to-carbon-fibre and biochar as engineered carbon black are emerging or unproven).

transformations.process: Purification and high-temperature graphitisation toward graphitic carbon
outputs: Graphite anode material for lithium-ion batteries
transformations.byproductFate: Residual carbon returns to soil-amendment or filtration use.
transformations.precedent: Biocarbon-to-battery-anode research
transformations.caveat: Downgraded from MODELLED: biomass char is predominantly a non-graphitising 'hard' carbon, so it resists conversion to battery-grade graphite even at high graphitisation temperatures. Biomass-derived hard carbon is a credible sodium-ion anode material, but 'high-purity graphitic carbon' for lithium-ion from biochar is not demonstrated and faces a genuine materials obstacle; commercial Li-ion anodes use synthetic or flake graphite.

transformations.process: Polymerisation of plant oils into bio-resins; composite fabrication (VARTM) with plant fibres
outputs: Bio-based polymers, epoxy resins, composite panels, pipes, filaments, turbine-blade skins, packaging, natural-rubber gaskets
transformations.byproductFate: Organic residue is composted or fed to the pyrolysis hub.
transformations.precedent: Bioplastics and natural-fibre composites are commercial
transformations.caveat: Core chemistry is real (epoxidised plant oils, bio-based resins and natural-fibre composites are commercial), but some listed outputs overreach: structural wind-turbine-blade skins exceed the stiffness and fatigue performance current bio-resins/natural-fibre laminates deliver, and natural rubber is tapped as Hevea latex, not made by polymerising plant oils.

transformations.input: Captured CO₂ (from carbothermic reduction, lime calcination, biomass combustion) + wastewater nutrients + seawater + sunlight
transformations.process: Algae photobioreactor cultivation
transformations.byproductFate: The reactor's inputs are themselves other processes' outputs: it consumes captured CO₂ and treats wastewater, closing carbon and water loops; spent biomass returns to pyrolysis or compost.
transformations.precedent: Algal biofuel and wastewater-treatment demonstrations
transformations.caveat: Algal biofuel economics remain unproven at scale; document treats CO₂ 'capture and utilise' generically without specifying capture technology.

transformations.process: Calcination (thermal decomposition to CaO): CaCO₃ → CaO + CO₂
outputs: Lime (CaO) for glass batch, zirconium-hydroxide precipitation, geopolymer, and marine carbonate reef additive
transformations.byproductFate: The calcination CO₂ feeds the capture-and-algae loop; the lime itself is a shared reagent across glass, zirconia and cement lines.
transformations.precedent: Lime kilning is a standard industrial process
transformations.caveat: Document hedges the feedstock as shells 'or limestone deposits, if available'; island limestone availability is uncertain.

transformations.input: Iron oxides (Ti-extraction byproduct) + TiO₂ (rutile) + carbon black (biochar) + plant-oil binder
transformations.process: Pigment grinding and paint compounding
outputs: Paints, coatings and art pigments (red/yellow iron oxides, TiO₂ white, carbon black)
transformations.byproductFate: This line IS the terminal fate of the titanium plant's iron-oxide byproduct and of biochar carbon, converting two 'wastes' into finished pigment.
transformations.precedent: Iron-oxide and TiO₂ pigment manufacture
transformations.caveat: Iron-oxide and TiO₂ pigment manufacture is real, but 'carbon black' is a specific engineered material made by controlled partial combustion / thermal decomposition of a hydrocarbon feedstock. Finely milled biochar can serve as a black pigment but is not equivalent to true carbon black in particle size, tint strength or consistency.

transformations.process: Collection, sorting and remelting
outputs: Recycled aluminium for solar-panel frames, wiring, cookware, utensils and structural parts
transformations.byproductFate: Recycled aluminium substitutes for bauxite smelting (saving up to 95% of the energy), and the metal is infinitely recyclable so it cycles indefinitely; dross reprocessed.
transformations.precedent: Aluminium can recycling (real, ~95% energy saving cited)

transformations.process: Crushing to cullet and remelting
transformations.byproductFate: Cullet re-enters the float-glass batch, lowering silica and energy demand.
transformations.precedent: Container-glass recycling
transformations.caveat: Document notes low market value (AUD 0.02-0.05/kg); driver is landfill/energy saving, not revenue. Container cullet also differs in composition from float-glass batch, so cross-use into solar cover glass needs colour/contaminant control.

transformations.process: Mechanical recycling / reprocessing
transformations.byproductFate: Reprocessed polymer feeds the additive-manufacturing filament stream.
transformations.precedent: PET/HDPE mechanical recycling

transformations.process: Urban mining: dismantling, leaching and metal recovery (acid leaching to strip contaminants)
outputs: Recovered copper, gold and rare-earth elements
transformations.byproductFate: Recovered Cu/Au/REEs re-enter the electronics, wiring and magnet lines, so end-of-life devices become feedstock for new ones.
transformations.precedent: E-waste recycling / urban mining is established
transformations.caveat: Household e-waste flows are small (document estimates ~5 kg/household/year) and mixed-metal recovery is chemically involved; REE recovery from e-waste specifically is far less mature than Cu/Au recovery.

transformations.process: Composting and anaerobic bio-digestion
transformations.byproductFate: Compost returns nutrients to greenhouse/outdoor soils; digestate is a fertiliser; biogas powers facilities, closing the food-energy-soil loop. Biochar is made separately in the pyrolysis hub, not by composting.
transformations.precedent: Anaerobic digesters and composting (Sundrop-style closed-loop agriculture cited)
transformations.caveat: Minor conflation in the original: composting and digestion yield compost and digestate, not biochar; biochar comes from the pyrolysis line.

transformations.process: Chemical vapour deposition (CVD) of diamond films
outputs: Diamond semiconductor films for high-power devices and radiation detectors
transformations.byproductFate: Not specified by the document.
transformations.precedent: CVD diamond growth is real lab/industrial practice
transformations.caveat: Doping diamond effectively (n-type especially, typically via phosphorus) remains difficult; document acknowledges this as a challenge, so diamond semiconductors stay largely at research stage.
